meta.intro: Six strategy iterations on the same 73-stock S&P 500 Tech universe. Two viable contenders (highlighted), four rejected research iterations (transparent disclosure).

S1.name: S1: Macro-Overlay Tech (pure momentum)
S1.description: Long-only top quartile by 12-1m momentum, gross exposure scaled by 4-component macro composite (VIX, credit, yield curve, SPX trend).
S1.verdict: PITCHED — highest CAGR (16.1%) and Calmar (0.57); cleanest narrative

S2c.name: S2c: Multi-Factor + Macro Overlay
S2c.description: Long-only top quartile by 2-factor composite (momentum + low-vol z-scores averaged), same macro overlay.
S2c.verdict: VIABLE — Sharpe 0.95, MaxDD -27%. Trades 3pp/yr CAGR for hair of Sharpe vs S1.

S2.name: S2: 3-factor L/S (original)
S2.description: Momentum + low-vol + reversal, L/S top vs bottom quintile, dollar-neutral.
S2.verdict: FAILED — reversal cancels momentum; L/S in tech is broken
S2.lesson: Reversal factor cancels momentum signal in the composite. Net result: dominated by low-vol, which alone underperforms in tech.

S2a.name: S2a: 2-factor L/S
S2a.description: Dropped reversal. Momentum + low-vol composite, L/S top vs bottom quintile.
S2a.verdict: FAILED — short side concentration in tech is the real problem
S2a.lesson: Cleaning up the signal isn't enough — the structural problem is the short side. Shorting 14 high-vol tech names during the AI rally creates concentrated tail risk.

S2b.name: S2b: 2-factor long-only (no overlay)
S2b.description: Dropped short side. Momentum + low-vol, long top quartile, no overlay.
S2b.verdict: Works (0.92 Sharpe) but -50% MaxDD; needs overlay
S2b.lesson: Long-only fixes the L/S problem (Sharpe jumps to 0.92), but without overlay the MaxDD is -50% — full tech beta exposure during crashes. Needs the regime overlay.

S3.name: S3: Tech + VIX L/S
S3.description: Pure momentum, L/S top vs bottom quartile within tech, gross exposure scaled by VIX level.
S3.verdict: Modest Sharpe (0.39); alpha not significant (t=0.17). VIX overlay too aggressive and L/S concentration drags.
S3.lesson: VIX is a useful regime signal but not enough as a single overlay. The full 4-component macro composite is materially better.

research_learnings.title: Reversal cancels momentum in composites
research_learnings.body: Equal-weighting a 12-month momentum signal with a 1-month reversal signal yields a portfolio dominated by the low-vol residual. Either factor alone outperforms the naive composite. Lesson: factor combination requires thought about signal correlation, not just stacking premia.

research_learnings.title: L/S in concentrated single-sector universes is broken
research_learnings.body: Tech has winner-take-all dynamics: 5-10 mega-caps drive most of the index's returns. Shorting 14-18 names in this universe creates unhedged tail risk. The 2018-2024 factor crash is well-documented and our results are consistent with the AQR / Two Sigma underperformance period.

research_learnings.title: Long-only + regime overlay > L/S in tech
research_learnings.body: Both S1 and S2c (long-only + macro overlay variants) deliver Sharpe ~0.93-0.95. None of the L/S variants achieved positive Sharpe. The structural choice (long-only vs L/S) dominates the signal choice (momentum vs multi-factor).

research_learnings.title: Macro overlay does what it says
research_learnings.body: Comparing S2b vs S2c: the macro overlay reduces MaxDD from -50% to -27% while costing ~6pp/yr of CAGR. For an institutional product, this trade-off is unambiguously worth it. The overlay specifically tames 2018-Q4 vol spike, COVID, and the 2022 bear.
